root_text.an3.110:1.1: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho anāthapiṇḍikaṁ gahapatiṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an3.110:1.2: “citte, gahapati, byāpanne kāyakammampi byāpannaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi byāpannaṁ hoti, manokammampi byāpannaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.110:1.3: Tassa byāpannakāyakammantassa byāpannavacīkammantassa byāpannamanokammantassa na bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, na bhaddikā kālakiriyā.
root_text.an3.110:1.4: Seyyathāpi, gahapati, kūṭāgāre ducchanne kūṭampi byāpannaṁ hoti, gopānasiyopi byāpannā honti, bhittipi byāpannā hoti;
root_text.an3.110:1.5: evamevaṁ kho, gahapati, citte byāpanne kāyakammampi byāpannaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi byāpannaṁ hoti, manokammampi byāpannaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.110:1.6: Tassa byāpannakāyakammantassa byāpannavacīkammantassa byāpannamanokammantassa na bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, na bhaddikā kālakiriyā.
root_text.an3.110:2.1: Citte, gahapati, abyāpanne kāyakammampi abyāpannaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi abyāpannaṁ hoti, manokammampi abyāpannaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.110:2.2: Tassa abyāpannakāyakammantassa abyāpannavacīkammantassa abyāpannamanokammantassa bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, bhaddikā kālakiriyā.
root_text.an3.110:2.3: Seyyathāpi, gahapati, kūṭāgāre succhanne kūṭampi abyāpannaṁ hoti, gopānasiyopi abyāpannā honti, bhittipi abyāpannā hoti;
root_text.an3.110:2.4: evamevaṁ kho, gahapati, citte abyāpanne kāyakammampi abyāpannaṁ hoti, vacīkammampi abyāpannaṁ hoti, manokammampi abyāpannaṁ hoti.
root_text.an3.110:2.5: Tassa abyāpannakāyakammantassa …pe… abyāpannamanokammantassa bhaddakaṁ maraṇaṁ hoti, bhaddikā kālakiriyā”ti.

translation_text.an3.110:1.1: Seated to one side, the Buddha said to the householder Anāthapiṇḍika:
translation_text.an3.110:1.2: “Householder, when the mind is fallen, bodily, verbal, and mental deeds are fallen.
translation_text.an3.110:1.3: Someone whose deeds of body, speech, and mind are fallen will not have a good death.
translation_text.an3.110:1.4: It’s like a bungalow with a bad roof. The roof peak, rafters, and walls fall in.
translation_text.an3.110:1.5: In the same way, when the mind is fallen, bodily, verbal, and mental deeds are fallen.
translation_text.an3.110:1.6: Someone whose deeds of body, speech, and mind are fallen will not have a good death.
translation_text.an3.110:2.1: When the mind is not fallen, bodily, verbal, and mental deeds are not fallen.
translation_text.an3.110:2.2: Someone whose deeds of body, speech, and mind are not fallen will have a good death.
translation_text.an3.110:2.3: It’s like a bungalow with a good roof. The roof peak, rafters, and walls are not fallen in.
translation_text.an3.110:2.4: In the same way, when the mind is not fallen, bodily, verbal, and mental deeds are not fallen.
translation_text.an3.110:2.5: Someone whose deeds of body, speech, and mind are not fallen will have a good death.”
